extraction.one_line_summary: TERPSTRA Niki attacked solo approximately 22-23km from finish after Quick-Step controlled positioning through critical bergs, held off disorganized chase.
tactical_patterns: Quick-Step neutralized team number disadvantage by controlling positioning through penultimate climbs before Terpstra's solo move.
LAMPAERT drove pace 25-30km out to string peloton, preventing organized chase group formation when Terpstra attacked.
Favorites' recon knowledge failed them; knowing parcours didn't translate to countering Quick-Step's positioning control.

rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: positive: 22-year-old showed unexpected diesel engine to hold solo from ~22km, suggesting underrated time-trial strength
rider_signals.VAN AVERMAET Greg: negative: pre-race favorite failed to position or respond when Terpstra moved, BMC early crash impacted race execution
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: positive: fourth place without visible attacks suggests strong positioning discipline, threat even when not animating
rider_signals.VANMARCKE Sep: positive: fifth confirms consistency in Flemish monuments, but never animated suggesting conservative team tactics
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: sixth after multiple placings reveals inability to win when Quick-Step controls tempo through bergs
